Core Gameplay Loop.observations: Tapping makes cows appear which is SO COOL but I don't know why I'm tapping or what the numbers mean. The graveyard button is scary and I don't want to press it. Why does a number go away?? I just want more cows. Tapping both sides is fun but confusing about left and right.

UX / Usability.observations: There are too many screens before the cows! Save slots? What is a save slot? I just want to see cows NOW. The pause button says II which doesn't mean anything to me. The graveyard buttons are at the bottom where my thumbs go and I keep pressing them by accident and then the number disappears and I get sad.

Visual Quality.observations: The grass is pretty and green! There is a red car! The cow is WHITE with SPOTS like a real cow and it BOBS UP AND DOWN which is the best thing I ever saw. The sky is nice. I like looking at all the colors. The gravestone is a little boring and grey.

Game Feel.observations: The cows come from far away and take 3 whole seconds which feels like FOREVER. I want the cow RIGHT NOW when I tap. No sounds at all makes it feel broken — where is the moo? Why is there no moo when the cow comes? Tapping and nothing happening right away makes me tap more and more everywhere.

Visual Correctness.observations: The cow looks like a real cow with spots and legs and a tail and it bobs which is very correct and good. The road looks like a road. The fence looks like a fence. Everything looks like what it is supposed to be which is good. Dust and speed lines are cool.

Performance Perception.observations: Everything moves smoothly when the cows walk toward me. No freezing or waiting that I notice. The cow bob animation is smooth. The game does not feel slow except for waiting 3 seconds for the cow to arrive which is too long for me.

top_strengths: The cow model is adorable with spots and a bobbing animation — instantly delightful for a young child
Tapping directly makes something visible happen on screen, which is satisfying once you understand it
Colorful 3D pastoral scene with grass, road, red car, and sky holds visual attention
top_issues: No sound effects at all — no moo when a cow appears is a huge missed opportunity and makes the game feel silent and broken to a child
Save slot screen before playing is a confusing gate — young children cannot read or understand save management and will be stuck or frustrated
3-second cow travel animation feels too slow; tapping with no immediate payoff causes confusion and random mashing, plus graveyard buttons placed where thumbs rest leads to accidental score resets
verdict: The bobbing spotted cow is pure magic for a young child, but the total silence, confusing save screen, and 3-second cow delay mean Milo will either hand the phone back or tap everything randomly in frustration within the first minute.
